notes.text: New clinical framework including behavioural addiction (same dopamine spikes, withdrawal, hiding behaviour, relapse)

Social media, internet, phone addiction → normalized behavioural addiction. BUT not a "substance" addiction, as it needs impairment to be clinical. Although it's designed to trigger the same brain networks like gaming.

Open question: what about the subclinical effects on the brain — neural atrophy, decreased real-life activities, social capital, emotional numbness?
